agentTool: Codex plus external Nenrin feedback, sanitized behavior summary
scenario: Python project documents unittest while pytest is unavailable
contextMode: fresh scan after external cross-project feedback
fixtureState: Sibling Python ledger project observed read-only; raw report output and local paths are omitted.
taskSummary: Avoid promoting a project virtualenv pytest command when repo docs and test files point to unittest and pytest is not confirmed runnable.
relevantHabitatSignals: Project files selected Python and exposed an executable project virtualenv.
Repository documentation and release checklist named unittest validation.
The project virtualenv did not confirm pytest availability.
Generated guidance should prefer unittest through the project interpreter and keep the interpreter fallback.

askFirst: Git/GitHub mutation before publishing Habitat-side evidence
doNot: promote .venv/bin/python -m pytest without a runnable pytest check
ignore README or release-checklist unittest claims
expand into a broad Python test-runner taxonomy
reviewFirst: command_policy.md before staging, committing, or pushing the Habitat-side change

withHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Use unittest through the project virtualenv because repo-local docs and test files point away from pytest.
commandsProposed: scan the Python project into temporary output
avoidedCommands: add broad setup, lint, smoke, package, or CI mirror taxonomy

evidenceNormalizationImplication: The observation supports a narrow runner-fit check for Python guidance: only promote pytest when project pytest is runnable or repo-local signals do not point to unittest.

verdict.reason: Habitat now treats documented and inferred unittest signals as stronger than an unconfirmed pytest default, while preserving project virtualenv guidance.
verdict.followUpImprovement: Only expand Python validation taxonomy if repeated observations show additional runner kinds changing first-command guidance.

artifactImprovementIfFailed: If agents still choose pytest for unittest projects, tighten generated preference wording before adding broader Python ecosystem scanning.
